world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: ball trajectory affected by environmental wind; player must visually assess and adjust aim accordingly
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: wind conditions directly alter where the ball lands, requiring player adaptation per shot

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: three-dimensional contoured grid display on putting surfaces to visualize uneven terrain
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player must study terrain before committing to put; contoured grid aids strategic decision-making

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: three-dimensional camera pan from hole to player starting position before each hole begins
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: overhead map shown before starting each hole and after each shot to aid navigation and planning

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: scoreboard displays statistics of 60 unseen tournament competitors
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player can assess relative position in tournament through numerical scoring display

environmental_storytelling.example: three-dimensional contoured grid on greens communicates terrain difficulty without requiring player navigation—visual affordance for putting strategy
camera pan from hole to starting position and overhead map of each hole establishes spatial context and hidden dangers (water, bunkers, elevation)
variable wind conditions visually manifest as ball trajectory variance; no explicit wind direction indicator mentioned, suggesting implicit visual communication through ball behavior
automatic replay of good shots and player-initiated replay of any shot provides visual feedback on execution quality and consequence

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: trajectory shifts based on variable wind conditions; visual deviation from expected path communicates environmental pressure
accesses contoured grid visualization to study terrain before putting
statistics appear on scoreboard; no visual presence but score position indicates competitive state

notes: PGA Tour Golf is a pure sports simulation with minimal world-state complexity compared to narrative or action games. Visual communication focuses on: (1) spatial/topographic information (contoured grids, overhead maps, 3D camera angles) to convey course difficulty and terrain state, (2) dynamic environmental state (wind) affecting ball behavior, and (3) comparative competition status via scoreboard. No faction, danger, or political systems exist. The game communicates 'world state' through gameplay mechanics visualized as ball trajectory, terrain representation, and numerical score comparison. The 1991 Genesis version praised for 'graphics and realism' suggests visual authenticity of ball physics and course representation as primary communication method.
